--- Thread (50 messages, 2024-05-05) ---
[2024-05-05 15:10] bleevoe: I think it's more important to agree on chooser always having advantage before we try to agree on whether my suggestion solves it.
[2024-05-05 15:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): "Ah shit, you rolled p1 in set variation #765443563447846791345, gg wp"
[2024-05-05 15:11] lyra1712 (Lyra): deterministic games are elaborate coinflips, it is known
[2024-05-05 15:12] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): and all games are deterministic on current consumer tech :LongNoseLiar:
[2024-05-05 15:14] silentslayers: Fair. I'm pretty disinterested in anything other than theoretically fair (omniscience scenario), so if a change is done to alleviate discomfort because of lack of experience, I'd personally be against it, at least for high level play.
[2024-05-05 15:14] bleevoe: Here's why chooser always has an advantage. Let's say it's a blind auction instead. Player A says P1 with gold on turn 3. Player B says P1 with gold on turn 5. Result: Player B gets P1 with gold on turn 5.

Same example, but this time Player A is cutter and says P1 with gold on turn 3. Chooser picks P1 with gold on turn 3 instead.
[2024-05-05 15:16] bleevoe: Which makes me realize that a blind auction seems like it makes everyone happy except xYotsu.
[2024-05-05 15:17] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Blind auction in omniscience is the same as no auction no?
[2024-05-05 15:17] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Unless you declare the game a tie if both bid the same thing
[2024-05-05 15:18] bleevoe: Yeah, you're right, didn't think about equal bids...
[2024-05-05 15:18] bleevoe: Just reroll the set at that point. 🙂
[2024-05-05 15:18] bleevoe: Wonderboat's omniscience is bound to fail sooner or later
[2024-05-05 15:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Maybe that's the simplest solution
[2024-05-05 15:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): You get 1 veto per x games
[2024-05-05 15:18] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Which rerolls the set
[2024-05-05 15:20] silentslayers: My counter argument to `Here's why chooser always has an advantage` is that it's possible both the cutter and the chooser are wrong here, and having relative differences in gold opinion doesn't help the chooser because both players can just have a wrong thesis anyways.
[2024-05-05 15:20] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But the chooser only has to be comfy with one of the options, the cutter has to be comfy with both
[2024-05-05 15:20] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Unless you are only evaluating logic and not emotion
[2024-05-05 15:22] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Also if we argue that the cutter can simply cut such that they are comfy with both, then I'd argue that they must exert much more effort to do so, compared to the chooser deciding what they are comfier with
[2024-05-05 15:23] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Because I'd argue that in human play "comfiness" is the main deciding factor in that system, not theoretical advantage
[2024-05-05 15:23] silentslayers: Well the chooser is already theoretically advantaged anyways
[2024-05-05 15:23] lyra1712 (Lyra): theres a simple strategy for the chooser to not be worse-on-average : flip a coin to decide which side to choose
[2024-05-05 15:23] silentslayers: Now they get this comfort win on top of it
[2024-05-05 15:23] lyra1712 (Lyra): theres nothing similar available for the cutter
[2024-05-05 15:24] silentslayers: Which side to choose what?
[2024-05-05 15:24] lyra1712 (Lyra): which side of the cut they choose to play
[2024-05-05 15:24] anesotericman (Esoteric): You can always have it be that both people look at the set, the first person to submit a betting line is the cutter, then the other person is the chooser.
[2024-05-05 15:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Even if all cutters propose instawin vs instalose, if you coinflip, you will at worst be 50% winrate
[2024-05-05 15:24] silentslayers: Yeah, interesting idea Lyra
[2024-05-05 15:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Assuming you don't throw your instawin sets :LongNoseLiar: :LongNoseLiar: :LongNoseLiar:
[2024-05-05 15:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But don't you want to be the chooser?
[2024-05-05 15:26] lyra1712 (Lyra): the cutter gets to set up baits tho : look at this big blue set, im cutting in such a way that lets you know i think p1 is advantaged (and pls don't look too closely at the galvani venges)
[2024-05-05 15:26] bleevoe: I like this. Combine it with the cutter's buff of getting gold 1 turn earlier to incentivize quick thinking.
[2024-05-05 15:26] anesotericman (Esoteric): Yeah, sorry cutter/chooser is wrong term for that one. More of a betting line the other person can either accept or play with slightly less advantage, one go.
[2024-05-05 15:27] anesotericman (Esoteric): No back-and-forth bidding and no side advantage
[2024-05-05 15:27] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): How does the betting work?
[2024-05-05 15:28] anesotericman (Esoteric): Player A and Player B both look at set at same time. A decides he could do palyer 2 with a +1 advantage on turn 4, but wouldn't do player 2 with a +1 advantage on turn 5. Player B gets to pick between (player 1 against the turn 4 advantage) or (player 2 with a turn 5 advantage)
[2024-05-05 15:30] silentslayers: Brain APM as a solution? Hell yeah
[2024-05-05 15:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Hmm not sure if I there's something I'm not understanding, but is this different from the cake-cutting approach?
[2024-05-05 15:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Oh because the chooser doesn't get to pick the better side?
[2024-05-05 15:31] bleevoe: Chooser's inherent advantage is gone
[2024-05-05 15:31] silentslayers: You get to be a buffed cutter if you brain APM
[2024-05-05 15:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In other words the cutter can veto the winning breakpoint
[2024-05-05 15:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So doesn't this flip the script then?
[2024-05-05 15:32] silentslayers: yes but rewards brain APM
[2024-05-05 15:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well I guess you decide by time
[2024-05-05 15:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And in omniscience it's a draw assuming equal input time
[2024-05-05 15:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In that they both bid the winning breakpoint at the same time
[2024-05-05 15:33] lyra1712 (Lyra): what happens if i bet 0, like i think the set is already fair
[2024-05-05 15:34] anesotericman (Esoteric): if neither player bids, it would just be random, which is fine, yeah

[2024-05-05 15:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Btw as a side-note, shouldn't the "slightly less" be 1 ply later?
[2024-05-05 15:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I.e +1 gold p2 turn x  --> +1 gold p1 turn x+1
[2024-05-05 15:35] anesotericman (Esoteric): you can only use gold on your own turn?
[2024-05-05 15:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or +1 gold p1 turn x --> +1 gold p2 turn x
[2024-05-05 15:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In your message you said +1 gold p2 turn x --> +1 gold p2 turn x+1 which skips p1 turn x+1
[2024-05-05 15:36] bleevoe: Are you talking about switching which player gets the extra gold? How does that make sense?
[2024-05-05 15:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): No I'm talking about the 2 options
[2024-05-05 15:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Shouldn't they be consecutive?
[2024-05-05 15:36] anesotericman (Esoteric): you're picking the player you think is disadvantaged in the set and saying the latest turn you'd accept an extra gold as compensation for the disadvantaged player
[2024-05-05 15:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Right but after p2 is p1's turn
[2024-05-05 15:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): If I am picking between p2 x and p2 x+1 then we skipped p1 x+1
[2024-05-05 15:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Which is between p2 x and p2 x+1
[2024-05-05 15:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): x being the turn number
[2024-05-05 15:37] anesotericman (Esoteric): If p2 is the disadvantaged side, the one that gets the extra gold, you only care about if they have the gold on their turn
[2024-05-05 15:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): right there can be sets like that
[2024-05-05 15:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): but there can also be sets where +1 gold on any turn flips the advantage in either player's favor
[2024-05-05 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): or at least +1 gold in the breakpoint's vicinity
[2024-05-05 15:39] bleevoe: How is P2 getting gold on turn P2 X + 1 different from P2 getting gold on turn P1 X + 1?
[2024-05-05 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): you get +1 gold on the player/turn of choice
[2024-05-05 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I'm just saying shouldn't the system be fully granular
[2024-05-05 15:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And the choices be consecutive?
[2024-05-05 15:39] lyra1712 (Lyra): i think you're getting confused
[2024-05-05 15:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): p1 t1, p2 t1
p1 t2, p2 t2
...
[2024-05-05 15:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): These are the turns in the game
[2024-05-05 15:40] w1tch0r: So If you think the sets are nearly completely fair you would say I play P1 if I get +1 gold in turn 20 or something. And then the other player can decide if he wants to play against that or take P1 with gold in turn 21.
[2024-05-05 15:40] lyra1712 (Lyra): the order is something like : p1+gold on turn 1 > ... > p1 + gold on turn 10 > even > p2 + gold on turn 10 > ... > p2 + gold on turn 1
[2024-05-05 15:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): There can be a set where if you give +1 gold to any player on any of those turns that player wins
[2024-05-05 15:40] lyra1712 (Lyra): in term of handicaps
[2024-05-05 15:41] silentslayers: imo SF is touching on the point of not enough granularity for fair cutting.
[2024-05-05 15:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Right but again you are saying p1 t20 --> p1 t21
[2024-05-05 15:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But that skips p2 t20
[2024-05-05 15:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): since the turns go

p1 t20  -- p2 t20 -- p1 t21
[2024-05-05 15:41] lyra1712 (Lyra): that a fair point, but his proposed semi solution is flawed/confused
[2024-05-05 15:41] lyra1712 (Lyra): unless i smoke too much
[2024-05-05 15:42] w1tch0r: Well if both agree p1 is the worse side it wouldnt make sense to give p2 gold
[2024-05-05 15:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): That's not true though
[2024-05-05 15:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Both agree that at +0 gold one side is worse
[2024-05-05 15:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): They don't say anything about +1 gold
[2024-05-05 15:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It can be that in +1 gold, whichever side gets the +1 gold is better
[2024-05-05 15:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And so the system should allow full granularity
[2024-05-05 15:43] lyra1712 (Lyra): whats your response to this @Spyrfyr https://discord.com/channels/112616041175089152/119275839652102145/1236689017366646824
[2024-05-05 15:43] w1tch0r: If you think they are so close to each other just bet on P1 with +1 in a million turns.
[2024-05-05 15:43] lyra1712 (Lyra): every option is given, and the order i gave is the order of handicaps
[2024-05-05 15:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yes that's the natural extension of my argument
[2024-05-05 15:43] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): My argument is just assuming that the breakpoints are consecutive
[2024-05-05 15:44] lyra1712 (Lyra): then i just dont understand your argument in the first place
[2024-05-05 15:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But you're right, that's not a general solution
[2024-05-05 15:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): The general solution is as you stated
[2024-05-05 15:44] siepu: dunno it all sounds both more convoluted and less interesting than just mulligan
[2024-05-05 15:44] lyra1712 (Lyra): theres no handicap between (p1 + gold on turn 10) and (p1 + gold on turn 11)

--- Thread (50 messages, 2024-05-05) ---
[2024-05-05 15:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well the general solution is as you stated, a set of inequalities on various turns for either side getting +1 gold
[2024-05-05 15:44] lyra1712 (Lyra): the handicap (p2 + gold on turn 10) is way off to the side of that
[2024-05-05 15:44] anesotericman (Esoteric): Yeah, I think mulligan is a good solution too.
[2024-05-05 15:44] silentslayers: Mulligan system + Just balancing the damn game more and playing bs+11 instead of bs+8 are pretty OP solutions, yeah
[2024-05-05 15:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And so the system should allow you to bid the top two inequalities
[2024-05-05 15:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Rather than just one side on a turn and that side on the next turn
[2024-05-05 15:45] lyra1712 (Lyra): but those are consecutive handicaps
[2024-05-05 15:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Since that may be skipping the 2nd best inequality if it involves the other side on some other turn
[2024-05-05 15:45] lyra1712 (Lyra): there are no inbetween
[2024-05-05 15:46] lyra1712 (Lyra): like maybe you could walk me through an example, coz i really dont get your point
[2024-05-05 15:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Actually sorry your equation is not assuming what I am saying
[2024-05-05 15:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): What I am saying is more like this:
[2024-05-05 15:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): pA +1 gold on turn x > pB + 1 gold on turn y > pC +1 gold on turn z > ...
[2024-05-05 15:47] lyra1712 (Lyra): but my claim is this can not happen
[2024-05-05 15:47] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Where A  B  C can be p1/2 and x/y/z are turn numbers
[2024-05-05 15:47] lyra1712 (Lyra): because somewhere there you have to put "no handicap" in there
[2024-05-05 15:47] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): There can be a set where p1 is winning by default

[2024-05-05 21:11] xyotsuba: Yes because to properly analyze a set you need to analyze it multiple times for the different gold adjustments. In order to properly read a set you have to memorize the openings for each of the different gold adjustments. There are already so many openings to learn.
[2024-05-05 21:38] .holyfire (Holy-Fire): Fischer introduced the concept of random chess because he didn't like the fact that so much time was spent on studying opening theory. The idea was that nobody would memorize openings for 960 different starting positions, and that the focus would be on opening *principles* rather than specific opening lines.

The same could work here. If there is a different gold adjustment each time, it would be impractical to memorize openings for each, and play would rely less on prior memorization and more on knowing the general principles and making deductions in real time.

That said, if people don’t like it I’m not opposed to other solutions - which one is best depends on the distribution of set imbalance.
